explanation: Caprylic/capric triglyceride is a lightweight ester derived from fractionated coconut oil, widely used as an emollient carrier that spreads easily and absorbs without greasiness. It is non-comedogenic, well-tolerated by all skin types including sensitive and oily, and functions as an excellent solvent for oil-soluble actives.

Caprylic/capric glycerides are a mixture of mono-, di-, and triglycerides of caprylic and capric fatty acids derived from fractionated coconut or palm kernel oil, used as lightweight emollients and emulsifiers. They are well-tolerated, non-comedogenic, and provide a smooth skin feel without the heaviness of full-chain coconut oil.

Caprylic/capric triglyceride is a lightweight ester derived from fractionated coconut oil and glycerin, one of the most widely used emollient carriers in modern skincare for its clean, non-greasy skin feel and excellent compatibility with actives. It is non-comedogenic, non-sensitizing, and well-tolerated across all skin types.

Capryloyl glycine is an amino acid-derived mild surfactant and antimicrobial agent used in gentle cleansers and toners for its ability to cleanse effectively at a skin-compatible pH while helping to control Cutibacterium acnes. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin and is often included in low-irritation cleansing formulas alongside other gentle surfactants.

Caprylyl glycol is a diol derived from caprylic acid (a coconut-sourced fatty acid) that functions as both a humectant and a preservative booster, drawing moisture to the skin while helping to inhibit microbial growth. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin and is frequently used as part of preservative systems in formulas positioned as paraben- and isothiazolinone-free.

Caprylyl methicone is a volatile silicone with a lighter, faster-evaporating feel than standard dimethicone, used as an emollient and carrier that imparts silkiness without the heavy or occlusive finish of longer-chain silicones. It is well-tolerated and suited to lightweight, fast-absorbing formulas for oily and combination skin types.

Caprylyl/capryl glucoside is a mild sugar-based surfactant derived from coconut fatty alcohols and glucose, used in gentle cleansers and foaming formulas for effective cleansing at a low irritation threshold. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin and is widely used in natural and clean-formula cleansers as a sulfate-free alternative.

Caramel is a natural brown colorant derived from heat-processed sugar, used in cosmetics to impart warm brown tones to foundations, self-tanners, and tinted products. It is considered non-sensitizing and well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Carbomer is a crosslinked polyacrylic acid polymer that, when neutralized with a base, swells to create the clear, gel-like texture found in many serums, sunscreens, and moisturizers. It is non-sensitizing, non-comedogenic, and well-tolerated across all skin types — one of the most widely used and extensively safety-tested thickeners in skincare.

Carica papaya (papaya) fruit extract contains papain, a proteolytic enzyme, along with vitamin C and antioxidant flavonoids that provide mild enzymatic exfoliation and brightening benefits. It is generally well-tolerated in rinse-off and low-concentration leave-on formulas, though the enzymatic activity can cause tingling or irritation on reactive, barrier-compromised, or papaya-sensitive skin.

Carnitine is an amino acid derivative naturally found in skin that plays a role in cellular fatty acid metabolism and is used in skincare as a conditioning agent and humectant. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and is included in some formulas for its potential to support skin energy metabolism and hydration.

Carnosine is a naturally occurring dipeptide antioxidant composed of beta-alanine and histidine, known for its ability to neutralize reactive carbonyl species and inhibit the glycation of skin proteins — a process that contributes to skin stiffness and loss of elasticity. It is well-tolerated and used in anti-aging formulas for its anti-glycation and free-radical-scavenging properties.

Carrageenan is a sulfated polysaccharide extracted from red seaweed (genus Chondrus and others), used in skincare as a thickener, gelling agent, and hydrating film-former. It is well-tolerated in topical formulas and provides a soft, moisturizing skin feel; concerns about its safety in food do not apply to cosmetic use at typical surface concentrations.

Carthamus tinctorius (safflower) seed oil is a lightweight plant oil with one of the highest linoleic acid contents (~75%) among vegetable oils, making it an excellent choice for supporting the skin's barrier lipid profile and reducing inflammation in dry or eczema-prone skin. It absorbs readily, has very low comedogenic potential, and is well-tolerated by sensitive and oily skin types alike.

Carum petroselinum (parsley) extract is a botanical antioxidant rich in apigenin, luteolin, and vitamin C that provides free-radical-scavenging and mild brightening benefits. It is well-tolerated and used in tone-evening and protective formulas for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory flavonoid content.

Carum petroselinum (parsley) extract is a botanical antioxidant containing apigenin, luteolin, and vitamin C with free-radical-scavenging and mild brightening properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and tone-evening formulas for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory flavonoid content.

Cassia tora (sickle senna) seed extract is a traditional Ayurvedic botanical providing polysaccharides and anthraquinone-type antioxidants with mild soothing and free-radical-scavenging properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and calming formulas for its antioxidant and hydration-supporting content.

Castanea crenata (Japanese chestnut) shell extract is a tannin-rich botanical antioxidant with strong free-radical-scavenging and mild astringent properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and pore-refining formulas for its polyphenol content and ability to neutralize oxidative stress.

Caulerpa lentillifera (sea grape) extract is a tropical green algae extract rich in polyphenols, polysaccharides, and omega-3 fatty acids with antioxidant and skin-conditioning properties. It is well-tolerated and used in marine-ingredient formulas for its ability to protect skin from environmental oxidative stress.

Cellulose is a plant-derived polysaccharide used in skincare as a thickener, film-former, and gentle physical exfoliant depending on particle size and form. It is inert, non-sensitizing, and well-tolerated across all skin types, and is one of the most abundant and sustainably sourced biopolymers used in cosmetic formulation.

Cellulose gum (carboxymethyl cellulose) is a water-soluble modified cellulose polymer used as a thickener, stabilizer, and film-former in skincare formulas, contributing smooth texture and mild moisture-retention. It is non-sensitizing, non-comedogenic, and well-tolerated across all skin types.

Centaurea cyanus (cornflower) flower extract is a mildly astringent botanical antioxidant rich in polyphenols and anthocyanins with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. It is well-tolerated and particularly used in eye-area and sensitive-skin formulas for its gentle calming and de-puffing effects.

Centella asiatica (gotu kola) is one of the most clinically supported botanical actives in skincare, containing triterpenoids — asiaticoside, madecassoside, asiatic acid, and madecassic acid — that stimulate collagen synthesis, calm inflammation, and accelerate barrier repair. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and is the foundational active in many K-beauty formulas for reactive, sensitized, and post-procedure skin.

Centella asiatica extract concentrates the plant's triterpenoid actives — asiaticoside, madecassoside, asiatic acid, and madecassic acid — which stimulate collagen synthesis, reduce inflammation, and accelerate barrier repair. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and one of the most widely validated botanical extracts for reactive, sensitized, and compromised skin.

Centella asiatica leaf extract delivers the plant's anti-inflammatory and collagen-stimulating triterpenoids (asiaticoside, madecassoside, asiatic acid) specifically from the leaf fraction. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and widely used in K-beauty and sensitive-skin formulas for calming reactive and compromised skin.

Centella asiatica leaf water is the hydrosol or water-phase distillate of centella asiatica leaves, delivering soluble triterpenoids and plant compounds with soothing and anti-inflammatory properties in a lightweight, water-based format. It is well-tolerated and used as a calming base in toners and essences for reactive and sensitive skin.

Cera microcristallina (microcrystalline wax) is a petroleum-derived refined wax used in skincare as a structuring and thickening agent in balms, sticks, and anhydrous formulas. It is considered inert and well-tolerated, providing a smooth, firm texture and mild occlusive barrier without significant sensitization risk.

Ceramide is a class of naturally occurring sphingolipids that form the structural backbone of the skin's lipid bilayer — the mortar between skin cells that holds the barrier together and prevents transepidermal water loss. Topically applied ceramides replenish depleted barrier lipids, reducing dryness, sensitivity, and irritant penetration; they are exceptionally well-tolerated and beneficial for all skin types, especially dry, reactive, and eczema-prone skin.

Ceramide 3 (ceramide NP) is the most abundant ceramide type in healthy human skin, playing a critical role in maintaining the integrity of the stratum corneum lipid bilayer. Topical ceramide 3 replenishes depleted barrier lipids to reduce dryness, sensitivity, and irritant penetration, and is exceptionally well-tolerated — a cornerstone ingredient for eczema-prone, reactive, and barrier-compromised skin.

Ceramide AP (ceramide 6-II) is a specific ceramide subtype containing an alpha-hydroxy fatty acid chain, one of the key ceramide classes found in human skin that helps maintain the stratum corneum's lamellar lipid structure. Topical ceramide AP supplements the natural ceramide pool to support barrier integrity, reduce water loss, and calm reactive or dry skin, and is exceptionally well-tolerated.
